revision.text:
[[en:Dannozuka Kofun]][[ja:段ノ塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name=段ノ塚古墳 |location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}忍坂字段ノ塚 |coordinates={{coordinates|34|30|27.71|N|135|52|32.42|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=段ノ塚古墳（伝舒明天皇陵）|qq=}} |type={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}（上八角下方墳） |built={{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}中頃 |area=八角対辺長約42m・下方前面約105m |Burial Facility=（伝）{{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |Interred Persons=（{{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}治定）第34代{{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|qid=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}} |陵墓={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}治定「押坂内陵」 |image=[[File:Mausoleum of Emperor Jomei.jpg|250px]]
段ノ塚古墳 |地図2={{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=30|coordinates=27.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=52|lon_sec=32.42|map_dot_label=段ノ塚古墳|mark={{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.svg|marksize=28|caption=|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|qq=}}| }} [[File:Mausoleum of Emperor Jomei.jpg|thumb]] {{ill|Dannozuka Kofun|ja|段ノ塚古墳|1=Dannozuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Dannozuka Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (段ノ塚古墳) is a [[Kofun]] in Japan. Specifically, it is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|lt=Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is possibly the tomb of {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|qid=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}{{Cite web |last=Hays |first=Jeffrey |title=ASUKA, FUJIWARA AND ASUKA-ERA CITIES AND TOMBS {{!|qq=}} Facts and Details |url=https://factsan11etails.com/japan/cat16/sub106/entry-5299.html#chapter-16 |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=factsan11etails.com |language=en|qq=}} and is of the same type as the attributed tomb of his son, {{ill|Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji|ja|御廟野古墳|1=Gobyō-no-Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mausoleum of Emperor Tenji|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyō-no-Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.{{Cite web |date=2023-10-16 |title=Dan-no-tsuka Kofun [Dannotsuka Kofun 段ノ塚古墳, Jomei Tennō-ryō Jomei Tennnoryo 舒明天皇] Passage Grave : The Megalithic Portal and Megalith map |url=https://www.megalithic.co.uk/article.php?sid=33044 |access-date=2023-10-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231016234831/https://www.megalithic.co.uk/article.php?sid=33044 |archive-date=2023-10-16 |qq=}} This kofun represents the continuation of elite burial practices during the Asuka period, with a type closely tied to imperial lineage.{{Cite web |title=Kofun Culture |url=http://www.t-net.ne.jp/~keally/kofun.html |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=www.t-net.ne.jp|qq=}} ==overview== '''Dannotsuka Kofun''' (段ノ塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en|Sakurai, Nara|qid=Q127026|lt=Sakurai, Nara|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The tumulus is an {{ill|Octagonal Kofun|ja|八角墳|1=Octagonal Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Octagonal Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. It is a late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} Kofun. The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} has Historic Site1 it as "'''押坂内陵''' (Osaka-no-uchi no misasagi)" and attributes it as the mausoleum of the 34th emperor, {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|qid=Q329761|lt=Emperor Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}. According to the Imperial Household Agency, the tumulus type is identified as an {{ill|Joenkahofun|ja|上円下方墳|1=Joenkahofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Joenkahofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/034/index.html 押坂内陵 宮内庁] == Overview == The tumulus is constructed at the southern tip of a ridge extending from Mount Goken. Farther into the valley behind the tumulus are the graves of {{ill|Princess_Kagami|en|Princess_Kagami|qid=Q11651292|lt=Princess Kagami|1=Princess Kagami}} and {{ill|Princess Ōtomo|ja|大伴皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ōtomo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also associated with the area. The tumulus mound is built by cutting through the ridge and constructing the Kofun on the slope. The adjacent valley areas have also been shaped, indicating that the Kofun construction involved extensive land modification.「段ノ塚古墳」『桜井の横穴式石室を訪ねて』 The tumulus features a unique structure: a trapezoidal base topped by an octagonal mound, often described as an "upper octagonal, lower square tumulus." The square base consists of three tiers, with the lowest front width measuring approximately 105 meters. Each tier is covered with natural stone facing. The octagonal section has two tiers, with the lower tier spanning approximately 42 meters across. {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} investigations revealed that the lower tier of the octagonal section is surfaced with flagstones (Haibara stones).「段ノ塚古墳」『桜井の横穴式石室を訪ねて』 The burial structure itself is not visible today, but an account from the late Edo period in the work "Sanryōkō" by Yamamori Kiyooki mentions two Sarcophaguss within the burial chamber, based on local testimonies. Furthermore, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} identified what appears to be a ceiling stone from a burial corridor (''yoshidō'') at the front of the octagonal section. If the chamber's layout follows standard kofun patterns, it could feature a colossal {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} exceeding 20 meters in length.「段ノ塚古墳」『桜井の横穴式石室を訪ねて』 Given its exceptional map_size and distinctive octagonal structure, Dannotsuka Kofun is highly likely to be the tomb of Emperor Jomei. The octagonal tumulus design emerged as a replacement for keyhole-shaped kofun, adopted by imperial families and prominent clans in the late Kofun period. Dannotsuka Kofun is historically significant as the first imperial Kofun to feature the octagonal design.「段ノ塚古墳」『桜井の横穴式石室を訪ねて』 == See Also == *{{ill|Late Kofun tumuli|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} == References == == External Links == * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/034/index.html 押坂内陵] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|段ノ塚古墳|version=103707815|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ceb:Dannozuka Kofun]] [[ceb:Dannozuka Kofun]] }} {{everybodywiki link|Dannozuka Kofun}} [[ceb:Dannozuka Kofun]] [[ja:段ノ塚古墳]] {{Commons category|Dannozuka Kofun}} [[en:Dannozuka Kofun]] {{wikidata link|Q18233688}} [[Category:Emperor Jomei]] [[Category:History of Sakurai, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial tombs]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Octagonal Kofun]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
